segments.text: Concerning the Ammonites, this is what the LORD says:
Has Israel no sons? Is he without heir? Why then has Milcom taken possession of Gad? Why have his people settled in their cities?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will sound the battle cryagainst Rabbah of the Ammonites. It will become a heap of ruins, and its villages will be burned. Then Israel will drive out their dispossessors, says the LORD.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wail, O Heshbon, for Ai has been destroyed; cry out, O daughters of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth and mourn; run back and forth within your walls, for Milcom will go into exiletogether with his priests and officials.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Why do you boast of your valleys—your valleys so fruitful, 
O faithless daughter? You trust in your riches and say,‘Who can come against me?’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Behold, I am about to bring terror upon you, declares the Lord GOD of Hosts, 
from all those around you. You will each be driven headlong, with no one to regather the fugitives.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Yet afterward I will restore the Ammonites from captivity,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Concerning Edom, this is what the LORD of Hosts says:

Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent?
Has their wisdom decayed?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Turn and run! Lie low, O dwellers of Dedan, for I will bring disaster on Esauat the time I punish him.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: If grape gatherers came to you, would they not leave some gleanings? Were thieves to come in the night, would they not steal only what they wanted?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text:
But I will strip Esau bare; I will uncover his hiding places, 
and he will be unable to conceal himself. His descendants will be destroyedalong with his relatives and neighbors, 
and he will be no more.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Abandon your orphans; I will preserve their lives. Let your widows trust in Me.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: For this is what the LORD says:
If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, can you possibly remain unpunished? You will not go unpunished, for you must drink it too.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For by Myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, that Bozrah will become a desolation, a disgrace, a ruin, and a curse, and all her cities will be in ruins forever.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I have heard a message from the LORD; an envoy has been sent to the nations:
Assemble yourselves to march against her! Rise up for battle!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For behold, I will make you small among nations, despised among men.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
The terror you cause and the pride of your heart
have deceived you, O dwellers in the clefts of the rocks,  O occupiers of the mountain summit. Though you elevate your nest like the eagle, even from there I will bring you down,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: declares the LORD.

Edom will become an object of horror. All who pass by will be appalled
and will scoff at all her wounds.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: As Sodom and Gomorrah were overthrownalong with their neighbors,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: says the LORD,
no one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Behold, one will come up like a lionfrom the thickets of the Jordan to the watered pasture. For in an instant I will chase Edom from her land. Who is the chosen one I will appoint for this? For who is like Me, and who can challenge Me? What shepherd can st and against Me?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore hear the plansthat the LORD has drawn up against Edom and the strategies He has devisedagainst the people of Teman: Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them.
At the sound of their fall the earth will quake; their cry will resound to the Red Sea.
Look! An eagle will soar and swoop down, spreading its wings over Bozrah. In that day the hearts of Edom’s mighty menwill be like the heart of a woman in labor.
Concerning Damascus:
Hamath and Arpad are put to shame, for they have heard a bad report; they are agitated like the sea;  their anxiety cannot be calmed.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Damascus has become feeble; she has turned to flee. Panic has gripped her; anguish and pain have seized her
like a woman in labor.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: How is the city of praise not forsaken, the town that brings Me joy?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For her young men will fall in the streets, and all her warriors will be silenced in that day,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD of Hosts.
I will set fire to the walls of Damascus; it will consume the fortresses of Ben-hadad.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon defeated, this is what the LORD says:
Rise up, advance against Kedar, and destroy the people of the east!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: They will take their tents and flocks, their tent curtains and all their goods. They will take their camels for themselves. They will shout to them: ‘Terror is on every side!’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Run! Escape quickly! Lie low, O residents of Hazor,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD,

for Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylonhas drawn up a plan against you; 
he has devised a strategy against you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, one that dwells securely,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
They have no gates or bars; they live alone.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Their camels will become plunder, and their large herds will be spoil. I will scatter to the wind in every directionthose who shave their temples; I will bring calamity on themfrom all sides,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Hazor will become a haunt for jackals,  a desolation forever. No one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: This is the word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah.
This is what the LORD of Hosts says:
Behold, I will shatter Elam’s bow, the mainstay of their might.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: I will bring the four winds against Elamfrom the four corners of the heavens, and I will scatter themto all these winds. There will not be a nationto which Elam’s exiles will not go.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: So I will shatter Elam before their foes, before those who seek their lives. I will bring disaster upon them, even My fierce anger,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: declares the LORD.
I will send out the sword after themuntil I finish them off.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: I will set My throne in Elam, and destroy its king and officials,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Yet in the last days, I will restore Elam from captivity,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: declares the LORD.
